2026-04-01 Reuters Tokyo stocks April 1 2026 market eye hot stocks broad rally

snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,134.67, up 2,070.95, while Prime-market breadth stood at 1,509 gainers, 44 losers and 14 unchanged.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3609.5, with overnight short covering spilling into cash trade.
USD/JPY stayed in the upper 158s, easing back from the 160.46 year-to-date low in the yen and softening part of the earlier intervention concern.
Brent was at $104.63 and WTI at $102.34. Oil is still elevated, but the panic tone has eased as hopes of an end to the war resurfaced.
The BOJ tankan showed large manufacturers at +17 and large non-manufacturers at +36, helping support cyclical and tech rebound buying.

drivers.driver: Broad short covering on de-escalation hopes and a U.S. rebound
drivers.why_today: Stronger U.S. equities and hopes that the Iran war could wind down helped the Nikkei retake the 53,000 area intraday. The first move is still a relief rally rather than fresh trend conviction.

drivers.driver: Tankan resilience and Korea's export strength are supporting tech and cyclicals
drivers.why_today: The BOJ tankan and strong Korean export data are reinforcing confidence in semiconductor and cyclical demand, which is feeding into the rebound in Japanese growth and export names.

drivers.driver: Buybacks, stake increases and earnings beats are driving the strongest stock-specific moves
drivers.why_today: Recruit, Zojirushi, Life Drink and KADOKAWA are leading because their catalysts are easy to understand and easy for the market to price.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3609.5
Asian equities rallied sharply and the Nikkei rose as much as 3.9%, with de-escalation hopes and strong economic data driving short covering
The dollar steadied and the yen recovered to 158.55, taking some immediate pressure off the 160 intervention zone
Brent was at $104.63 and WTI at $102.34, showing that oil remains elevated even as outright panic eased
At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 53,134.67 (+2,070.95), with Prime-market breadth at 1,509 gainers versus 44 losers
The BOJ tankan showed large manufacturers at +17 and large non-manufacturers at +36, with sentiment still proving firmer than feared
Recruit Holdings approved a buyback capped at 4.58% of shares outstanding and 350 billion yen
Recruit Holdings rose as much as 7.6% as the market welcomed the new buyback
Zojirushi Mahobin reported 23.5% growth in first-quarter ordinary profit
Zojirushi Mahobin became the top Prime-market gainer, rising as much as 11.2% on the strong quarterly result
Life Drink Company jumped more than 11% after Iris Ohyama raised its stake to 7.70%
KADOKAWA rose more than 4% after Oasis increased its holding to 13.76%
KDDI fell more than 5% after lowering guidance in the wake of its accounting scandal

watchlist.name: Recruit Holdings
watchlist.why_today: The company approved a buyback capped at 4.58% of shares outstanding and 350 billion yen. Even in a broad rally, this is one of the clearest and most liquid stock-specific catalysts on the board.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading above the 7,000-yen area into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding the size of the buyback cleanly
Can the stock retain buyers even if the broader rebound cools
falsifiers: The opening reaction fades quickly once short covering is done
Large-cap profit-taking starts dominating the tape
watchlist.red_team: The setup is strong, but mega-caps can still lose momentum quickly after an opening gap.

watchlist.name: Zojirushi Corporation
watchlist.why_today: A 28% increase in Q1 operating profit drove an intraday jump of more than 11%, making it the clearest earnings reaction in the Prime market this morning.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 1,650-yen area and stay near the highs
Does the market keep pricing upside to the full-year view despite no formal raise
Can real dip-buying volume continue into the afternoon
falsifiers: The stock starts filling the gap lower after the opening surge
Investors decide the strong Q1 is not enough to change the full-year case
watchlist.red_team: The quarter was strong, but without a full-year raise the initial burst can still cool quickly.

Can the market continue to price a restructuring or strategic angle

Volume dries up quickly after the morning burst

deep_dive.title: Recruit Holdings: the key question is whether the buyback story can keep carrying after the opening surge

lines: The core of the move is the supply-improvement story created by a buyback capped at 4.58% of shares and 350 billion yen.
Even in a broad rebound, a liquid large-cap with a clean capital-allocation catalyst can continue to attract differentiated demand.
After the March 31 close, the company disclosed the new buyback, and Reuters reported that the stock rose as much as 7.6% in morning trade on April 1.
By late morning it was still holding above the 7,000-yen area, showing that the headline had not yet fully faded.
Can it stay above the 7,000-yen area rather than fading after the opening burst.
If the index rally cools, does turnover remain stock-specific enough to keep the name firm.
Profit-taking in large caps quickly overwhelms the buyback logic.
The stock starts acting like pure index beta once the first reaction is over.
Because it is a mega-cap, short-term money can rotate out quickly after a strong opening gap.
If Middle East headlines or FX swings re-take control, the stock-specific strength can get buried.

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei gives back most of the 11:00 JST rebound and turns decisively lower again
If crude spikes again and reverses the improving intraday sentiment
If USD/JPY jumps back toward 160 or intervention-style volatility returns suddenly
If top catalyst names such as Recruit and Zojirushi fail to hold their morning high zones and break lower on real volume
